quote_of_day.text: >>19080 >we are simply growing up I've fallen very low at some times in my life, but I never would mock rape victims. If you ever mocked rape victims there is something very intrinsically wrong with your head, the cure of which would be shotgun shell directly to it.
quote_of_day.name: Ambedkar

guest_letters.title: Based website
guest_letters.body: Why aren’t we talking more about this IAC guy?? I just woke uop and checked bahut interesting site hai, there’s quotes and funny articles and board status?? We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Narthie

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of sacrilegious remarks moved over the board, bringing scattered thunderstorms of blasphemy and dietary law debates.

A cold front of disdain for sacred animals blew in, with one anon remarking that having a sacred animal was 'retarded' and eating beef was acceptable.

A heat wave of racial tension developed in the afternoon, as an anon sparked debate by comparing Sub-Saharan Africa to India, drawing a rash of incendiary comments.

Meanwhile, a light drizzle of suggestions for random names instead of IDs fell across the board, with one user proposing that users have a 'stickedup name' for each thread.

A dark fog of caste hatred rolled in late in the evening, with an anon calling for violence against low castes and another post teasing a 'gobarjeeta kumari girl group', prompting reactions from the audience.

The atmosphere remained tense, with no clear clearing in sight.

articles.title: Anon debates sacred animals, dietary laws, and faith
articles.body:
Ambedkar started the thread by criticizing the concept of sacred animals in culture, specifically targeting the reverence for cows. He argued that eating beef is rational and that cows are just animals, not deities.

Anon responded by suggesting that the disagreement stems from different frameworks and moral layers that people apply. They noted that even if one doesn't care about animal welfare, the spiritual layer of their beliefs is a separate issue.

Another anon clarified that in their perspective, cows are not considered God, but rather an animal meant to be protected. They mentioned that the concept of Aghniya, or protected cows, has been misunderstood. The discussion also touched on genetics and dietary laws.

Anon countered the claim that the Y chromosome is weaker than the X, calling it a common myth. They explained that X and Y chromosomes evolved differently but are fundamentally the same.

Narthie later expressed frustration with the thread, stating that it was initially decent but got ruined by off-topic comments. They criticized users for deviating from the subject and making personal attacks.

Anon also discussed their plans to recreate the Indus Valley Diet with a focus on meat, seafood, and low-glycemic index carbohydrates. They mentioned that this diet would have physiological consequences and aimed to make it a part of their lifestyle.

The thread included various opinions on faith, morality, and dietary choices. Anon noted that choosing not to harm animals is a moral stance, while following dietary laws is more about faith.

Haggu and other users engaged in a side conversation about genetics, IQ, and the history of certain populations. These exchanges included claims about the Ashkenazi Jewish population and their genetic history.

Throughout the thread, users shared their perspectives on the intersection of faith, culture, and personal choices. The discussion remained focused on the ideas presented, with users responding to each other's points and counterpoints.

articles.title: Anon compares Sub-Saharan Africa to India, sparking debate
articles.body:
Soythie started the discussion by stating that Sub-Saharan Africans are "unhinged" and have "0 impulse control like a homo erectus," comparing India's accomplishments unfavorably to the potential consequences of an influx of Sub-Saharan Africans.

Kantabai Ownes weighed in, referencing a documentary about the Central African Republic war and Russian alliances, and made a general statement about the incompetence of "nigger leaders." Yui made multiple posts, including one where they fantasized about a sexual scenario involving Candace Owens and another where they discussed the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.

Chuddie chimed in with a comment about the potential risks of "contamination" with "negro blood" and its effects on IQ.

Nikhil Fuentes made several posts, including one where they claimed that one-third of the Negro genome is "non-sapien" and another where they fantasized about a personal "ebony servant." The conversation also touched on topics such as Israel's actions in Uganda and the idea that Sub-Saharan Africans are not capable of self-governance.

Soythie responded to some of the comments, stating that India is objectively better than Sub-Saharan Africa despite its challenges.

articles.title: Anon Suggests Random Names Instead of IDs
articles.body:
Fuwawa proposed replacing IDs with random names for users, suggesting a list of names like Dyaush, Haggu, and Lungi. The OP asked others to suggest more names.

Anon Momojeet responded, criticizing the initial list as "kino" and "pajeetry," and requested more varied names. Momojeet also suggested ensuring that the same name is not used by multiple users.

Soyjeet dismissed the idea as "dogshit" and proposed a system with lists of first and last names to create random combinations, such as Ramesh Patel or Vikram Singh. Fuwawa replied, suggesting that having more names would be better to avoid duplication.

Aizen suggested adding names like Ambedkar and Aizen himself, while Bhondu requested names like Gura, Fuwawa, and Mococo. Soyjeet later jokingly asked if the name would be unique in each thread. Nepajeet suggested adding Aryanon and Psuedoanon, while Momojeet suggested Gilgamesh.

Aizen made a comment about Ozyrandias being honorary Aryan. Narthie jokingly made a comment about having "cute puppies" and "biting Khalistanis." The conversation included various suggestions and criticisms of the proposed system.

articles.title: Ambedkar teases gobarjeeta girl group, audience reacts
articles.body:
Ambedkar asked if readers' sisters were auditioning for an upcoming gobarjeeta girl group.

The poster expressed interest in seeing "pyaari behenas" from "ananmas" singing and dancing to "cringey but catchy zoomer songs." Repliers discussed the hypothetical group's prospects and the appeal of idols.

Dyaush mentioned "NEpyari supremacy" and fondly recalled a "momo wali pyaari /nep/yaari didi." Another poster, Lungi, described a disparaging image of a potential gobarjeeta idol. Some respondents fantasized about relationships with idols.

Mococo and Fuwawa discussed the appeal of secretly dating an idol, with Mococo graphically detailing a scenario. Haggu made several comments, including one about "larp and bait" and another criticizing idolatry.

articles.title: Anon calls for violence against low castes
articles.body: Soythie opened the thread, saying 'Why aren’t hating low castes today? Skin low castes alive!!' Anon Mococo responded, outlining criteria for identifying low castes and expressing a desire to 'mow them the fuck away.' Anon Bhondu stated that 'caste kanging will only stop when we rightfully take back the lands from them,' and listed several violent and discriminatory actions against low castes.
